english: rgyal-poi z'al-nas (rgyal po'i zhal nas) gsuńs (gsungs) the king spoke Glr. frq.; z'al-gyi sgo (zhal gyi sgo) the door of the face, the mouth (cf. also _dzúm-pa ('dzum pa)); z'al-la mi nyán-pa (zhal la mi nyan pa) Glr. to be disobedient; z'al-gyis bz'és-pa (zhal gyis bzhes pa) Glr. or _č'é-ba ('che ba) Sch. to promise, and other significations of k'as lén-pa (khas len pa), e.g. to accept Tar. 126, 10; z'al bgrád-pa (zhal bgrad pa) and gdáń-ba (gdang ba) to gape Sch., _byéd-pa ('byed pa) to open the mouth, z'al _dzúm-pa (zhal 'dzum pa) to smile; with ltá-ba (lta ba) 1. z'al-la ltá-ba (zhal la lta ba), e.g. č'os smrá-bai (chos smra ba'i) to watch the mouth of the preacher, to hang on his lips Pth.; in a similar manner: gús-pai séms-kyis ńá-yi z'al-la lta (gus pa'i sems kyis nga yi zhal la lta) Pth. 2. z'al ltá-ba (zhal lta ba), z'al-lta byéd-pa (zhal lta byed pa) to serve (v. z'al-ta (zhal ta)), z'al yań kyéd-la lta mc'ód-pa yań kyéd-la _bul (zhal yang khyed la lta mchod pa yang khyed la 'bul) they serve you and honour you Glr.; z'al-lta-ru byuń (zhal lta ru byung) he came to serve him Mil.; z'al gdáms-pa (zhal gdams pa) to bid, order, exhort Glr.; z'al dón-pa (zhal don pa) to pronounce, to deliver, state, report; z'al mjál-ba (zhal mjal ba) Mil. to visit, to come to see; z'al mt'óń-ba (zhal mthong ba) to see a person's face Tar.; 'in order to attain the highest dńos-grúb (dngos grub), one must séms-kyi rań-z'al mt'óń-ba (sems kyi rang zhal mthong ba), and in order to be able to do this, one must penetrate into the Buddhist doctrine' — thus Mil. teaches a Bonpa; pyis z'al mt'óń-bao (phyis zhal mthong ba'o) afterwards his face was seen, he made his appearance Tar.; z'al-gzígs-pa (zhal gzigs pa) v. sbst. z'al-gzíg (zhal gzig). Comp. For the most part expressions of civility: z'al-kár (zhal dkar), resp. for kar-yól (dkar yol) plates and drinking-vessels. — z'al-dkyil (zhal dkyil) face Cs. — z'al-bkód (zhal bkod) order, ordinance Sch.(?). — z'al-skóm (zhal skom), z'ai-skyéms (zhai skyems) drink. — z'al-skyin (zhal skyin) Glr. countenance. — z'al-skyógs (zhal skyogs) cup, goblet Mil. — z'al-k'ébs (zhal khebs) cover of an image of Buddha Sch. — z'al-k'rid (zhal khrid) oral or personal instruction Mil. — z'al-_k'ań (zhal 'khang) biting words of a superior (Sch. prob. not quite correct). — *z'al-gyá* (*zhal gya*) (rgya? (rgya?) brgya (brgya)) *)č'é-pa ('che pa) or z'é-pa* (zhe pa) to promise C. — z'al-rgyán (zhal rgyan) mustaches C. — z'al-ńó (zhal ngo) 1. = ńo (ngo), z'al-ńó nág-par bz'ugs (zhal ngo nag par bzhugs) he was sitting there with a mournful face Glr. 2. ts'ogs-č'en-z'al-ńó (tshogs chen zhal ngo) title of the chief-justices of the great monasteries of Sera, Gadan and Depung. 3. Sch.: 'z'al-ńo (zhal ngo) or ńor (ngor) (?), noble sons, princes' (?) — z'al-dńós (zhal dngos) bodily, in one's own body or person, sańs-rgyás z'al-dńós-kyi _K'rúńs-yul (sangs rgyas zhal dngos kyi 'khrungs yul) Pth., the place where Buddha was born bodily; z'al-dńós-su mjál-bar yod (zhal dngos su mjal bar yod) Glr. he is bodily to be seen. — z'al-sńa (zhal sna) Cs. = spyan-sńa (spyan sna). — z'al-čól (zhal col) resp. for 'ar-čól (ar col) handkerchief, napkin C. — z'al-č'ád (zhal chad) v. k'a-č'ád (kha chad). — z'al-č'u (zhal chu), z'al-č'áb (zhal chab) Schr., Cs. spittle, saliva. — z'al-mc'ú (zhal mchu) lip, v. k'a-mc'ú (kha mchu). — z'al-č'é (zhal che) judgment, decision; des _ú-bu-čag-yi z'al-č'é gc'ód-do (des 'u bu cag yi zhal che gcod do) he shall pass sentence on us Dzl. 282, 15, and elsewh. (the text of Sch. is not quite correct); z'al-č'é bču-drúg-pa (zhal che bcu drug pa) and bču-gsúm-pa (bcu gsum pa) ('the code) with the 16 and that with the 13 judgments'; these are two distinct bodies of law, both of them in C. of standard authority; z'al-č'é-pa (zhal che pa) judge Dzl. — z'al-č'éms (zhal chems) v. č'ems (chems) 2. — z'al-nyód (zhal nyod) favourite dish Sch. — z'al-ta (zhal ta) 1. also z'al-lta (zhal lta) a. service, turn. b. inspection, visitation, revision; z'al-ta byéd-pa (zhal ta byed pa) a. to serve, b. to inspect, review, superintend; to visit, the poor, the sick and to take care of them; to guard, z'iń-la (zhing la) the field. 2. resp. for k'á-ta (kha ta), k'a-gdáms (kha gdams) direction, instruction, counsel, advice, z'al-ta z'ib-rgyás z'ú-ba (zhal ta zhib rgyas zhu ba) to ask for accurate and detailed instructions Mil.; z'al-ta-pa (zhal ta pa) = sku-mdún-pa (sku mdun pa), z'ábs-p'yi (zhabs phyi) waiting-man, 'alet-de-chambre C., Tar. 56,2: servant in a convent; more frq. fem., z'al-ta-ma (zhal ta ma) waiting-woman, lady's maid, chamber-maid. — z'al-gdáms (zhal gdams) instruction, advice, jig-rtén-la dgós-pai z'al-gdáms gsúńs-so ('jig rten la dgos pa'i zhal gdams gsungs so) he imparted to her useful maxims Glr.; order, command Glr. (v. above); also, z'al-gdáms bris-mk'an (zhal gdams bris mkhan) author, in as much as all printed books are considered to be sacred, and the authors generally are Lamas, whose words are looked upon as divine. — z'al-gdóń (zhal gdong) countenance. — z'al-bdág (zhal bdag) in large religious meetings a Lama, who walks about with a wand in order to preserve good order, a verger. — z'al-_débs (zhal 'debs) a free-will offering or present Cs. — z'al-lpágs (zhal lpags) lip. — z'al-p'yís (zhal phyis) resp. napkin. — z'al-bád (zhal bad) (or pad? (pad?)) C. chief overseer, superintendent. — z'al-byáń (zhal byang) title, superscription, inscription. — z'al-ts'óm (zhal tshom) (for og-ts'óm ('og tshom)) Pth. beard. — z'al-ts'ós (zhal tshos) Sch. (Cs. z'al-ts'us (zhal tshus)) = z'al-zás (zhal zas) Dzl. food. — z'al-zág (zhal zag) tobacco-pipe, v. gań-zag (gang zag). — z'al-gzígs (zhal gzigs) 1. looking with the face, lhor (lhor), southward Glr. 2. apparition, z'al-gzígs t'ób-pa (zhal gzigs thob pa) to see an ap-
